spt_programs.programName: 4-Week Foundation Strength Cycle — Post-Menopausal Bone & Muscle Protocol
spt_programs.progressionJustification: DUP rotates heavy (3–5 rep), moderate (6–8 rep), and volume (10–12 rep) sessions across the week, simultaneously driving neural adaptations for strength and metabolic stress for hypertrophy — both critical for sarcopenia prevention and bone density in post-menopausal women. Because no prior maxes are recorded, the first two weeks function as a conservative load-calibration phase before meaningful intensity increases in weeks 3–4.
spt_programs.weeklyVolumeNotes: GLUTES_HAMSTRINGS receives 11 sets/week spread across all 3 sessions (hip thrust 2×/week + RDL + KB swing accessories). UPPER_BACK_ERECTORS gets 9 sets across all 3 sessions via barbell rows, DB rows, and cable rows as secondaries. QUAD_DOMINANT receives 9 sets across sessions 1 and 2 as primary and secondary slots. PUSH gets 6 sets across sessions 2 and 3. VERTICAL_PULL gets 6 sets across sessions 2 and 3. UNILATERAL_LOWER gets 6 sets per leg across all 3 sessions (2 per session). CORE gets 6 sets across all 3 sessions (2 per session). CARRIES_LOADED appear once in session 3 each week as a mandatory loaded carry finisher.

sessions.label: Workout 1 for the week starting June 28, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Posterior Chain Heavy + Quad Volume

warmup.name: Rowing machine easy pace
warmup.notes: Low intensity, focus on breathing and posture — spine tall, shoulders relaxed

warmup.name: Hip 90/90 stretch
warmup.notes: Sit on floor, front and back leg at 90°; press through floor to rotate torso toward front shin

warmup.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
warmup.notes: Place roller at mid-back, arms across chest; gently extend over roller, pause 2 s at bottom; inch up 3–4 positions

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Light band; keep arms straight; squeeze scapulae together at end range — rotator cuff activation

warmup.name: Band external rotation (side-lying or standing)
warmup.notes: Elbow at 90°; rotate forearm away from body; 2-second hold at end range

warmup.name: Glute bridge bodyweight activation
warmup.notes: Squeeze glutes hard at top; hold 1 s; prime the posterior chain before deadlifts

warmup.name: Goblet squat with pause (empty KB)
warmup.notes: 3-second pause at bottom; cue elbows up, chest tall; open hips and prepare quads

strength.coachingNotes: DUP Heavy day. Start conservative — select a load that feels like RPE 7 at most; technique is the priority in week 1. Brace hard, hinge at hips, push floor away. No touch-and-go; full reset each rep. Increase load slightly if form is solid by set 3.

Superset with the barbell row (exercise 3). Non-competing muscle groups allow concurrent performance. Moderate load — hips crease below parallel if mobility allows; otherwise stop at parallel. Brace and brace again before each rep.

Superset with back squat (exercise 2). Hinge to 45° torso angle; pull bar to lower ribs; squeeze shoulder blades hard at top. Conservative load in week 1 — choose a weight where the last 2 reps are a challenge but form stays perfect.

RPE 7–8 — start with 10–12 kg DBs each hand for week 1. Rear foot elevated on a bench; front foot far enough forward that the knee stays behind toes. Superset with Pallof press (exercise 5) — alternate sides to save time.

Use a cable stack or resistance band anchored at chest height. Start light — RPE 7; the core should work hard to resist rotation. Press hands straight out, pause 2 s, return. Superset with split squat (exercise 4).

metcon.name: Swing & Row
metcon.description: 14-minute AMRAP. Complete as many rounds as possible of: 12 KB swings, 10 push-ups, 200 m row machine, 10 step-ups (5 each leg, bodyweight or light DBs). Move steadily — this is an aerobic effort, not a sprint. Rest briefly when form degrades.

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Hip hinge drive; keep lumbar neutral; bell to eye level at most

movements.name: push-up
movements.notes: From toes or knees — choose a version you can complete unbroken; full range of motion

movements.name: row machine
movements.notes: Moderate pace — damper 4–5; drive with legs first then lean back

movements.name: step-up
movements.notes: Box or bench at knee height; step fully through at top; bodyweight only or hold 5–8 kg DBs if comfortable

mobility.name: Lat stretch — doorframe or rig hang
mobility.notes: Grip rig at shoulder height, step back, shift hips away; feel the stretch along the side of the torso; breathe into tight spots

mobility.name: Thoracic rotation in quadruped
mobility.notes: Hand behind head; rotate elbow toward the sky; follow with eyes; pause at end range; keep hips level

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch (shoulder IR)
mobility.notes: Lie on side, arm at 90°; gently push forearm toward floor to improve internal rotation; never force — mild tension only

sessions.label: Workout 2 for the week starting June 28, 2026
sessions.focus: Upper Body — Push & Vertical Pull + Glute Accessory

warmup.name: Air bike easy
warmup.notes: Easy pace — warm up the shoulders and thoracic spine; breathe through the nose

warmup.name: Y/T/W raises (prone on bench or floor, light or bodyweight)
warmup.notes: Control each movement; pause 2 s at top; scapular retraction and depression are the goal — rotator cuff prehab

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Light band; arms straight; squeeze shoulder blades at end

warmup.name: Cat-cow thoracic mobilisation
warmup.notes: Slow and deliberate; try to isolate thoracic extension, not just lumbar

warmup.name: Lat pulldown (cable, very light)
warmup.notes: Warm-up sets only; feel the lats engage; prep for working sets

warmup.name: Hip thrust bodyweight activation
warmup.notes: Pause 2 s at top; ensure glutes — not lower back — are doing the work

strength.coachingNotes: DUP Moderate day — leading with push. Landmine preferred over barbell overhead press for shoulder safety given limited shoulder internal rotation. Stand tall; press at 45° angle; brace core; controlled eccentric 3 seconds down. Both hands on the sleeve — bilateral press. Conservative load week 1.

Superset with hip thrust (exercise 3). Non-competing muscle groups — upper back vs. glutes. Pull bar to upper chest; keep chest tall; avoid shrugging. Lead with elbows driving toward hips. 2-second pause at bottom of movement.

Superset with lat pulldown (exercise 2). Upper back on bench; barbell padded across hip crease; drive hips to full extension; squeeze glutes at top 2 seconds. Chin tucked — avoid hyperextending the lumbar. Start conservative week 1 — hip thrust is a cornerstone of this cycle for bone loading.
RPE 7–8 — choose approximately 16–20 kg DB to start in week 1. Brace on bench; pull elbow high; think 'row the elbow, not the hand'. Superset with reverse lunge (exercise 5).

RPE 7 — start with 10–12 kg DBs each hand week 1. Step back, lower rear knee toward floor without touching; front knee tracks over second toe; upright torso. Superset with single-arm row (exercise 4).
Bodyweight. Press lower back into floor throughout; extend opposite arm and leg slowly; exhale on extension; pause 1 s at full extension. Quality over speed — this is anti-extension core stability.

metcon.name: EMOM Triple Threat
metcon.description: 15-minute EMOM. Rotate through 3 movements: Minute 1 = 10 goblet squats, Minute 2 = 10 ring rows, Minute 3 = 5 med ball slams. Repeat 5 rounds. Choose weights so you finish each minute with 15–20 seconds rest. Log loads for next week.

movements.name: goblet squat
movements.notes: Hold KB at chest; elbows in; sit between knees; drive through heels to stand

movements.name: ring row
movements.notes: Adjust body angle to make 10 reps challenging but unbroken; squeeze shoulder blades at top

movements.name: med ball slam
movements.notes: Reach overhead; slam hard into floor; catch on bounce or pick up; hinge on the pick-up, not flex the spine

mobility.name: Child's pose with arm reach (lat focus)
mobility.notes: Walk hand to one side; pull hip opposite way; breathe into the lat

mobility.name: Thread-the-needle thoracic rotation
mobility.notes: From quadruped; slide one arm under body; allow thoracic rotation; keep hips level

mobility.name: Cross-body shoulder stretch (IR focus)
mobility.notes: Pull elbow across chest; use opposite hand to deepen; keep shoulder blade down

sessions.label: Workout 3 for the week starting June 28, 2026
sessions.focus: Full Body — Squat Volume + Upper Pull + Carries

warmup.name: Ski erg easy
warmup.notes: Light pull, arms only first 60 s then full body; prime thoracic extension and shoulder girdle

warmup.name: Wall slide with foam roller (shoulder IR mobility)
warmup.notes: Stand against wall, arms at 90°; slide arms up wall keeping forearms in contact; corrects limited IR and thoracic stiffness

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff activation — light band, straight arms, retract scapulae at end

warmup.name: Hip flexor stretch (lunge position)
warmup.notes: Rear knee on ground; drive hips forward; tuck pelvis slightly; release psoas before squatting

warmup.name: Goblet squat with 3-second pause (light KB)
warmup.notes: Warm up the squat pattern with mobility emphasis; open hips at bottom

strength.coachingNotes: DUP Volume day — leading with squat pattern (not hinge — satisfies alternating-lead constraint after session 1 hinge and session 2 push). High rep, moderate load; hold KB at chest. Excellent for bone loading and quad hypertrophy. Choose a KB that makes sets 3–4 challenging. Consider 20–24 kg as starting point.

Superset with DB row (exercise 3). Hinge at hips; slight knee bend; lower bar along shins until hamstring tension; no rounding of lower back. Conservative load week 1 — this is a secondary after the squat primary. NOTE: this is the only hinge this session (hinge cap = 2 per session; 1 used here as secondary).

Superset with RDL (exercise 2). Non-competing muscle groups. Hinge to 45°; pull DBs to hips; squeeze scapulae. Choose a weight that challenges at rep 8–10. Approximately 14–18 kg each DB as starting point week 1.

RPE 7 — start with 10–12 kg DBs each hand. Box at knee height; drive through the elevated heel; avoid pushing off the rear foot. Superset with face pull / band pull-apart (exercise 5) for time efficiency.

strength.movement: Band Pull-Apart with External Rotation Emphasis
strength.coachingNotes: Light band. Arms straight; pull band to chest level; at end range rotate hands outward (supinate); hold 2 s. Superset with step-up (exercise 4). Targets rear deltoid and upper back — critical for posture given thoracic stiffness.

Mandatory loaded carry — appears once this week. Choose 20–24 kg KBs per hand; shoulders packed; walk with purpose. Grip, core, and bone loading all in one. Increase load when 30 m feels easy. Rest 90 s between sets.
Brace as if bracing for a punch; squeeze glutes; don't let hips sag or pike. Progress to 40 s in weeks 3–4 at the same effort. Final exercise of the session — finish strong.

metcon.name: Full Send Friday
metcon.description: For Time — 4 rounds of: 15/12 cal air bike, 12 DB floor press, 8 pull-ups (or ring rows as scale), 10 alternating reverse lunges. Record total time. Aim to keep each round within 30 s of the first.

movements.name: air bike
movements.notes: Women: 12 cal; push the pace but keep it sustainable for 4 rounds

movements.name: DB floor press
movements.notes: Lie on floor; DBs at chest; press to lockout; controlled lowering — floor limits ROM and protects shoulders

movements.name: pull-up
movements.notes: Use band assistance if needed; full hang to chin over bar; scale to ring rows if no pull-up yet

movements.name: lunge
movements.notes: Bodyweight; step forward, lower rear knee near floor, drive back to standing; stay tall

mobility.name: Doorframe lat stretch
mobility.notes: Grip frame at hip height; walk feet back; shift hips away; feel the pull along the lat

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: 3–4 positions along mid-back; arms across chest; exhale as you extend over the roller

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR focus — gentle pressure only; never force; breathe into the stretch

sessions.label: Workout 1 for the week starting July 5, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Posterior Chain Heavy + Quad Volume

warmup.name: Rowing machine easy pace
warmup.notes: Build to a comfortable pace; focus on posture — chest open, shoulders back

warmup.name: Hip 90/90 stretch
warmup.notes: Same as week 1; note any improvement in range

warmup.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
warmup.notes: 3–4 positions; exhale at each position

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff activation — light band

warmup.name: Y/T/W raises (prone on bench)
warmup.notes: Scapular stability — hold each rep 2 s at top

warmup.name: Glute bridge with 2-second hold
warmup.notes: Posterior chain primer before deadlift

strength.coachingNotes: Week 2 — add approximately 2.5–5 kg to week 1 working weight if RPE was ≤7 last session. DUP Heavy day continues. 4 working sets of 4; still conservative but beginning to push into meaningful intensity. Reset fully between reps.
Superset with barbell row (exercise 3). Add 2.5 kg from week 1 if form was solid. Controlled descent; pause briefly at bottom; drive through heels.
Superset with back squat (exercise 2). Add 2.5 kg from week 1 if form was solid. Same cues — pull to lower ribs, squeeze scapulae, 45° hinge.
RPE 7–8 — maintain the same load as week 1 (approximately 10–12 kg each hand); focus on technique refinement. Weeks 3–4 add 1 rep per set at same load. Superset with Pallof press (exercise 5).
Same load and reps as week 1; ensure technique is solid before adding weight. 2-second pause on each press-out. Superset with split squat (exercise 4).

metcon.name: Rounds of Reckoning
metcon.description: 5 Rounds For Time: 15 KB swings, 10 push-ups, 250 m ski erg, 10 hanging knee raises. Rest as needed between rounds but aim to beat your slowest round by the end. Log total time.

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Same KB as week 1 — aim for unbroken sets; hinge, not squat

movements.name: push-up
movements.notes: Maintain same standard as week 1; from toes if possible

movements.name: ski erg
movements.notes: Drive from hips and lats; consistent pace across 5 rounds

movements.name: hanging knee raise
movements.notes: From bar or rings; control the descent; avoid swinging

mobility.name: Lat stretch — doorframe or rig
mobility.notes: Same protocol as week 1

mobility.name: Thoracic rotation in quadruped
mobility.notes: Move slowly; maximise rotation at each rep

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR — note any improvement vs week 1

sessions.label: Workout 2 for the week starting July 5, 2026
sessions.focus: Upper Body — Push & Vertical Pull + Glute Accessory

warmup.name: Air bike easy
warmup.notes: Warm up shoulders and thoracic; moderate resistance

warmup.name: Y/T/W raises (prone)
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff — hold 2 s at top of each rep

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Light band; retract scapulae

warmup.name: Cat-cow thoracic mobilisation
warmup.notes: Slow; isolate thoracic not just lumbar

warmup.name: Hip thrust bodyweight with 2-second hold
warmup.notes: Glute activation before hip thrust working sets

strength.coachingNotes: DUP Moderate day. Add approximately 2.5 kg from week 1 if last session felt like RPE ≤7. Continue bilateral landmine press. Controlled 3-second descent. Brace the core hard throughout.
Superset with hip thrust (exercise 3). Add 2.5 kg from week 1 if form was solid. Pull to upper chest; lead with elbows; 2-second pause.
Superset with lat pulldown (exercise 2). Add 5 kg from week 1 if glutes were working and form was solid. 2-second hold at the top. This is the primary glute stimulus this session.
Same load as week 1 (approximately 16–20 kg); weeks 3–4 add 1 rep per set. Focus on scapular retraction. Superset with reverse lunge (exercise 5).
Same load as week 1 (approximately 10–12 kg each hand); maintain form. Superset with single-arm row (exercise 4).
Bodyweight. Same as week 1 — prioritise pressing lower back into floor throughout the movement. No rush.

metcon.name: Tabata Grind
metcon.description: 4 rounds of Tabata (8 intervals of 20 s work / 10 s rest) cycling through 4 movements: air bike, ring row, step-up, push-up. Each movement gets 2 consecutive Tabata intervals before rotating. Score = total reps. Note: no skill movements in Tabata per protocol rules.

movements.name: air bike
movements.notes: Max calories in 20 s; 2 intervals before rotating

movements.name: ring row
movements.notes: Max reps in 20 s; adjust angle to maintain quality; 2 intervals

movements.name: step-up
movements.notes: Bodyweight or light DBs; alternate legs; 2 intervals

movements.name: push-up
movements.notes: From toes or knees; full range of motion; 2 intervals

mobility.name: Child's pose with arm reach (lat focus)
mobility.notes: Walk hand further this week; breathe into the lat

mobility.name: Thread-the-needle thoracic rotation
mobility.notes: Attempt to get more rotation than week 1

mobility.name: Cross-body shoulder stretch
mobility.notes: Deepen stretch slightly if comfortable

sessions.label: Workout 3 for the week starting July 5, 2026
sessions.focus: Full Body — Squat Volume + Upper Pull + Carries

warmup.name: Ski erg easy
warmup.notes: Full body warm-up; focus on thoracic extension during pull phase

warmup.name: Wall slide with foam roller
warmup.notes: Shoulder IR and thoracic prime — same as week 1

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff activation

warmup.name: Hip flexor lunge stretch
warmup.notes: Release psoas before heavy squatting

warmup.name: Goblet squat pause (light KB)
warmup.notes: Mobility and movement prep

strength.coachingNotes: DUP Volume day. Add 2–4 kg to the KB from week 1 if last session felt manageable at RPE 7. Keep tempo controlled — 3-second descent. Excellent for bone loading via compressive forces.
Superset with DB row (exercise 3). Add 2.5 kg from week 1. Focus on the stretch in hamstrings; don't push range beyond where back can stay neutral. One hinge this session (RDL) — hinge cap respected.
Superset with RDL (exercise 2). Add 2 kg from week 1. Same 45° hinge; pull to hips; squeeze at top.
Same load as week 1 (approximately 10–12 kg each hand); technical focus. Superset with band pull-apart with ER emphasis (exercise 5).

strength.movement: Band Pull-Apart with External Rotation Emphasis
strength.coachingNotes: Same band and technique as week 1. Focus on the rotation at end range. Superset with step-up (exercise 4).
Add 2–4 kg per hand vs week 1 if grip and posture held. Mandatory loaded carry. Shoulders packed; walk tall.
Push toward 40 s if 30 s felt easy in week 1. Brace and squeeze glutes throughout.

metcon.name: Saturday Eve AMRAP
metcon.description: 14-minute AMRAP: 200 m row machine, 10 DB floor press, 12 goblet squats, 15 sit-ups. Move continuously but not recklessly — this is the end of your training week; save something in the tank for recovery.

movements.name: row machine
movements.notes: Consistent pace; same damper setting as earlier in the week

movements.name: DB floor press
movements.notes: Same load as week 1 session 3 — push for quality reps

movements.name: goblet squat
movements.notes: Same KB as EMOM week 1 — drive elbows up; sit tall

movements.name: sit-up
movements.notes: Anchor feet or use AbMat; full range; control the descent

mobility.name: Doorframe lat stretch
mobility.notes: End of week — spend extra time here; breathe deeply

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: 5–6 positions today; extra time as weekly cooldown

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Slightly longer than usual — end-of-week shoulder recovery

sessions.label: Workout 1 for the week starting July 12, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Posterior Chain Heavy + Quad Volume

warmup.name: Rowing machine easy
warmup.notes: Notice any weekend stiffness; extra focus on hip hinge warm-up

warmup.name: Hip 90/90 stretch
warmup.notes: Week 3 — you may feel more opening; explore the range

warmup.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
warmup.notes: Standard protocol; 3–4 positions

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff activation

warmup.name: Y/T/W raises
warmup.notes: Scapular stability prep; hold 2 s at top

warmup.name: Glute bridge with resistance band above knees
warmup.notes: Add band to increase glute med activation before deadlifts

strength.coachingNotes: Week 3 — intensity increases. DUP Heavy: 3 reps, higher percentage. Add 5–7.5 kg from week 2 working weight if RPE was ≤8. This should feel heavy but not maximal — rate 8 RPE target. Full 4-minute rest between sets. This is where bone-loading mechanical stress peaks in the cycle.
Superset with barbell row (exercise 3). Weeks 3–4: add 2.5–5 kg from block 1 average. Same depth and cues — the goal is progressive overload.
Superset with back squat (exercise 2). Weeks 3–4: add 2.5 kg from block 1. Maintain hinge angle; rows should feel heavier than weeks 1–2.
Week 3 progression: add 1 rep per set vs weeks 1–2 (now 13 reps). Same load as weeks 1–2 (approximately 10–12 kg each hand). Superset with Pallof press (exercise 5).
Week 3 progression: add 1 rep per set (now 13 reps). Same load as weeks 1–2. 2-second pause. Superset with split squat (exercise 4).

metcon.name: Slam & Pull
metcon.description: For Time — 3 rounds: 250 m ski erg, 10 KB swings, 8 pull-ups, 6 med ball slams. Then 2 rounds: 250 m ski erg, 12 KB swings, 10 pull-ups, 8 med ball slams. Record total time.

movements.name: ski erg
movements.notes: Drive hard; maintain pace across all 5 rounds

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Hinge and snap; lumbar neutral; bell to eye level

movements.name: pull-up
movements.notes: Band assist if needed; full hang each rep; scale to ring rows

movements.name: med ball slam
movements.notes: Reach overhead; slam; hinge to retrieve — do NOT round the spine to pick it up

mobility.name: Lat stretch — doorframe or rig
mobility.notes: Week 3 — focus on breathing into restriction; try to feel more release than week 1

mobility.name: Thoracic rotation in quadruped
mobility.notes: Increase to 10 reps this week; maximise rotation at each

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Assess whether IR has improved vs week 1; note progress

sessions.label: Workout 2 for the week starting July 12, 2026
sessions.focus: Upper Body — Push & Vertical Pull + Glute Accessory

warmup.name: Air bike easy
warmup.notes: Thoracic prime — push handles back and open the chest

warmup.name: Y/T/W raises (prone)
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff — add light DBs (1–2 kg) if bodyweight feels easy

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Light band — activation only

warmup.name: Cat-cow thoracic mobilisation
warmup.notes: Slower today — maximise each segment

warmup.name: Hip thrust bodyweight pause holds
warmup.notes: 3-second hold at top; glute activation before working sets

strength.coachingNotes: Week 3 — add 2.5 kg from week 2. DUP Moderate day continues. Focus on power out of the bottom — think 'punch through'. Controlled eccentric remains 3 seconds.
Superset with hip thrust (exercise 3). Add 2.5 kg from week 2. Squeeze lats at bottom; 2-second pause.
Superset with lat pulldown (exercise 2). Add 5 kg from week 2. 2-second hold at top; chin tucked; ribs down. Week 3 hip thrust should feel challenging by set 4.
Week 3 progression: add 1 rep per set (now 13 reps). Same load as weeks 1–2 (approximately 16–20 kg). Superset with reverse lunge (exercise 5).
Week 3 progression: add 1 rep per set (now 13 reps). Same load as weeks 1–2 (approximately 10–12 kg each hand). Superset with single-arm row (exercise 4).
Week 3 progression: add 1 rep per side (now 11 reps). Bodyweight. Same technique emphasis — lower back pressed to floor throughout.

metcon.name: EMOM Push-Lunge
metcon.description: 15-minute EMOM. Minute 1 = 12 cal air bike, Minute 2 = 10 push-ups, Minute 3 = 10 alternating reverse lunges (5 each leg). Repeat 5 rounds. Note: no skill movements in EMOM. Score = rounds completed without breaking into the rest period.

movements.name: air bike
movements.notes: Women 10 cal; push and pull the handles; strong legs

movements.name: push-up
movements.notes: Maintain same standard throughout all 5 rounds; from toes if possible

movements.name: lunge
movements.notes: Bodyweight; step back rather than forward to reduce knee stress; torso upright

movements.name: hanging knee raise
movements.notes: Controlled descent; if grip fatigues use straps; sub ab mat sit-ups if needed

mobility.name: Child's pose with arm reach (lat)
mobility.notes: Week 3 — walk hand further; actively breathe into restriction

mobility.name: Thread-the-needle thoracic rotation
mobility.notes: Increase to 10 reps; maximise rotation

mobility.name: Cross-body shoulder stretch
mobility.notes: Deepen slowly; note any restriction change vs earlier weeks

sessions.label: Workout 3 for the week starting July 12, 2026
sessions.focus: Full Body — Squat Volume + Upper Pull + Carries + Balance

warmup.name: Ski erg easy
warmup.notes: Full body; thoracic emphasis on pull phase

warmup.name: Wall slide with foam roller
warmup.notes: Shoulder IR prime

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff

warmup.name: Single-leg stance balance drill
warmup.notes: Mandatory balance element — stand on one leg; eyes open first then close if comfortable; proprioceptive challenge

warmup.name: Goblet squat pause (moderate KB)
warmup.notes: Movement prep — slightly heavier than week 1 warm-up

strength.coachingNotes: DUP Volume day. Week 3 — add 2–4 kg to the KB from week 2. Maintain 3-second descent. At this load, sets 3–4 should be genuinely challenging — that's the stimulus for bone density and hypertrophy.
Superset with DB row (exercise 3). Add 2.5–5 kg from week 2. Hamstring stretch is the cue — lower only as far as neutral spine allows. One hinge this session — hinge cap respected.
Superset with RDL (exercise 2). Add 2 kg from week 2. Pull to hips; squeeze scapulae.
Week 3 progression: add 1 rep per set (now 13 reps). Same load as weeks 1–2 (approximately 10–12 kg each hand). Superset with band pull-apart ER (exercise 5).

strength.movement: Band Pull-Apart with External Rotation Emphasis
strength.coachingNotes: Week 3 progression: add 1 rep (now 16 reps). Same band. Superset with step-up (exercise 4).

Mandatory loaded carry. Week 3 variant: suitcase carry (single side) to challenge lateral core stability. Choose 24–28 kg KB; hold at side; resist the lean; walk tall. 20 m per side per set. Increases anti-lateral-flexion demand.
Week 3 progression: hold for 40 s. Same form cues — brace, squeeze glutes, flat back.

metcon.name: Row & Ring
metcon.description: 5 Rounds For Time: 250 m row machine, 10 ring rows, 12 step-ups (6 each leg), 15 sit-ups. Record total time and compare to week 1 session 1 AMRAP as a proxy for fitness change.

movements.name: row machine
movements.notes: Consistent split across 5 rounds; damper 4–5

movements.name: ring row
movements.notes: Challenging angle — feet forward enough to make it work; squeeze at top

movements.name: step-up
movements.notes: Bodyweight or light DBs (5–8 kg each hand); drive through heel at top

movements.name: sit-up
movements.notes: Full range; hands at temples; controlled descent

mobility.name: Doorframe lat stretch
mobility.notes: Week 3 — maximise the hold; breathe deeply for 5 full breaths each side

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: 5–6 positions; linger at the stiffest segments

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Push into the restriction gently; note whether it has improved vs week 1

sessions.label: Workout 1 for the week starting July 19, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Posterior Chain Peak + Quad Volume

warmup.name: Rowing machine easy
warmup.notes: Final week — focus on feeling ready, not fatigued; prime the CNS gently

warmup.name: Hip 90/90 stretch
warmup.notes: Note any improvement in range vs week 1

warmup.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
warmup.notes: 4 positions; thoracic prep for deadlift bracing

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff activation

warmup.name: Y/T/W raises
warmup.notes: Scapular stability; light or bodyweight

warmup.name: Glute bridge with band and 3-second hold
warmup.notes: Full posterior chain prime before peak week deadlifts

strength.coachingNotes: Week 4 — peak heavy day. Add 2.5–5 kg from week 3 if RPE was ≤8. Target RPE 8.5 on set 4 — the heaviest single working load of the cycle. Full 4-minute rest between sets. This is a meaningful bone-loading stimulus. Do NOT attempt more than planned — technique preservation is non-negotiable.
Superset with barbell row (exercise 3). Final week — add 2.5 kg from week 3. Controlled and strong — demonstrate mastery of the movement.
Superset with back squat (exercise 2). Final week — add 2.5 kg from week 3. End-of-cycle record the total load moved — this becomes the baseline for the next program.
Week 4 progression: same 13 reps as week 3. Add 2 kg per DB if 13 reps felt smooth at RPE ≤7 in week 3. Superset with Pallof press (exercise 5).
Week 4: add 1 rep (now 14) or a small load increase if week 3 felt easy. 2-second pause every rep. Superset with split squat (exercise 4).

metcon.name: AMRAP Finish Line
metcon.description: 14-minute AMRAP: 250 m ski erg, 10 DB floor press, 8 pull-ups, 12 goblet squats. This is the final week — aim for more rounds than week 1 session 1. Record your score.

movements.name: ski erg
movements.notes: Hard effort; this is the start of each round — set the pace

movements.name: DB floor press
movements.notes: Slightly heavier than week 1 if comfortable; full ROM; control the descent

movements.name: pull-up
movements.notes: Aim for unbroken sets; band if needed; chin clearly over bar

movements.name: goblet squat
movements.notes: Heavier KB than week 1 if comfortable; sit tall; drive through heels

mobility.name: Lat stretch — doorframe or rig
mobility.notes: Final week — extra hold time; breathe into restriction for 6+ breaths each side

mobility.name: Thoracic rotation in quadruped
mobility.notes: Full 10 reps; maximise rotation; compare to week 1 range

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Note total improvement in shoulder IR across the 4 weeks

sessions.label: Workout 2 for the week starting July 19, 2026
sessions.focus: Upper Body — Push & Vertical Pull + Glute Accessory

warmup.name: Air bike easy
warmup.notes: Final week prep — thoracic and shoulder focus

warmup.name: Y/T/W raises (prone, light DBs)
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff with added load if tolerated — 1–2 kg

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Activation — light band

warmup.name: Wrist flexor and extensor stretch
warmup.notes: Mandatory wrist/forearm element this week — extend arm, bend wrist up then down; 45 s each

warmup.name: Hip thrust bodyweight 3-second holds
warmup.notes: Glute prime before working sets

strength.coachingNotes: Week 4 — add 2.5 kg from week 3. Final week DUP Moderate day. Focus on quality and power. This load should be the heaviest moderate-day press of the cycle. Record it for the next program's starting point.
Superset with hip thrust (exercise 3). Add 2.5 kg from week 3. Final week — the pull should feel strong. Record the load.

Superset with lat pulldown (exercise 2). Add 5 kg from week 3 if able. Peak hip thrust load of the cycle — this is a significant bone-loading event at the hip. 2-second hold at full extension. Record the load for next program.
Week 4: add 1–2 kg if 13 reps in week 3 felt smooth at RPE ≤7. Choose approximately 18–22 kg DB. Superset with reverse lunge (exercise 5).
Week 4: add 1–2 kg per DB if week 3 felt easy. Choose approximately 12–14 kg each hand. Superset with single-arm row (exercise 4).
Week 4 progression: 12 reps per side. Bodyweight. Final week — focus on perfect execution; this movement has been building anti-extension core stability across all 4 weeks.

metcon.name: For Time: Finale
metcon.description: For Time — 5 rounds: 15/12 cal air bike, 15 KB swings, 10 push-ups, 10 alternating reverse lunges. This is the final week push — aim for consistent round splits. Record total time and compare to week 1.

movements.name: air bike
movements.notes: Women: 12 cal; 5 rounds — pace yourself but push hard

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Heavier than week 1 if comfortable; hip snap; bell to eye level

movements.name: push-up
movements.notes: From toes; full range; maintain standard from week 1

movements.name: lunge
movements.notes: Bodyweight; step back to reduce knee stress; upright torso

mobility.name: Child's pose with arm reach (lat)
mobility.notes: Final week — breathe for 6+ breaths each side; maximise the stretch

mobility.name: Thread-the-needle thoracic rotation
mobility.notes: Compare range of motion to week 1 — you should notice improvement

mobility.name: Cross-body shoulder stretch
mobility.notes: Final week — hold longer; compare IR restriction to week 1

sessions.label: Workout 3 for the week starting July 19, 2026
sessions.focus: Full Body — Squat Volume Peak + Upper Pull + Carries + Plyometric

warmup.name: Ski erg easy
warmup.notes: Final session of the 4-week cycle — prime thoroughly

warmup.name: Wall slide with foam roller
warmup.notes: Shoulder IR and thoracic prep

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff activation

warmup.name: Low pogo hops on soft surface (low-impact plyometric)
warmup.notes: Mandatory plyometric element — gentle, small hops on a yoga mat or rubber flooring; stay light on your feet; prepare the bone and tendon for loading

warmup.name: Single-leg balance with perturbation (coach taps or band)
warmup.notes: Mandatory balance element — stand on one leg while coach gently perturbs balance; eyes open; improve proprioception

warmup.name: Goblet squat pause (heavy-ish warm-up)
warmup.notes: Prep for the peak volume day goblet squat; use 80% of planned working load

strength.coachingNotes: DUP Volume day — peak week. Add 2–4 kg from week 3 KB. This is the heaviest goblet squat of the cycle; sets 3–4 should be an 8/10 effort. Record the KB weight as your baseline for the next program's volume day starting point.
Superset with DB row (exercise 3). Add 2.5–5 kg from week 3. Final RDL of the cycle — use it to reinforce perfect hip hinge mechanics. One hinge this session — hinge cap respected.
Superset with RDL (exercise 2). Add 2 kg from week 3. Record the load — this becomes the week 1 secondary reference for the next cycle.
Week 4: add 1–2 kg per DB if week 3 felt smooth. Choose approximately 12–14 kg each hand. Superset with band pull-apart ER (exercise 5).

strength.movement: Band Pull-Apart with External Rotation Emphasis
strength.coachingNotes: Week 4: same 16 reps as week 3; add a slightly stronger band if 16 reps felt very easy. Superset with step-up (exercise 4).

Mandatory loaded carry — final week. Increase distance to 40 m and/or increase load by 2–4 kg per hand vs week 3 if grip and posture held. End-of-cycle benchmark: record KB weight and note whether 40 m felt manageable. Shoulders packed; walk with purpose.
Final week progression: 45 s. Compare to week 1 (30–40 s). Record this number — the next program will start here. Perfect form throughout; never sacrifice position for time.

metcon.name: Tabata Closer
metcon.description: Tabata-style: 8 intervals of 20 s work / 10 s rest per movement, cycling through 4 movements — row machine, ring row, med ball slam, sit-up. Each movement gets 2 consecutive Tabata intervals before rotating. Score = total reps across all movements. Note: no skill movements in Tabata per protocol.

movements.name: row machine
movements.notes: Max metres in 20 s; 2 intervals; powerful drive; final week — push harder than week 1

movements.name: ring row
movements.notes: Max reps in 20 s; 2 intervals; more challenging angle than week 2 if possible

movements.name: med ball slam
movements.notes: Max reps in 20 s; 2 intervals; reach fully overhead each rep; hinge to retrieve — mandatory low-impact plyometric element

movements.name: sit-up
movements.notes: Max reps in 20 s; 2 intervals; full range; controlled on the way down; compare score to week 2 session 6 AMRAP sit-up volume

mobility.name: Lat stretch — doorframe or rig
mobility.notes: End of the full 4-week cycle — maximum hold time; breathe for 6–8 breaths each side

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: 5–6 positions; take your time; celebrate how much your thoracic mobility has improved

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Final shoulder IR stretch of the cycle — note total improvement since week 1 and report to coach for next program design
